title: Test tcp deadlock fixes
body:
This fix follows #14667 and #14886

Several tests pipelined large numbers of commands on deferring clients without draining replies. That can fill buffers and stall progress.

Fix by draining replies every 500 pipelined requests to avoid TCP stalls.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: changes are limited to test/helpers scripts, adjusting pipelining/read patterns to avoid socket buffer stalls without affecting production code paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents test hangs caused by pipelining large numbers of commands on deferring clients without draining replies.
> 
> Updates several Tcl tests/helpers to **periodically read/discard replies (generally every 500 requests)**, adds final reply-draining in `gen_write_load`, and tweaks batching parameters in `memefficiency.tcl` to reduce buffer pressure during large write bursts.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0192dbf14f8827641ef38118b6b1f0ce6b88c648. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Fix COMMAND GETKEYS for PFMERGE with no source keys
body:
PFMERGE's second key spec (source keys) produces an empty range when called with only a dest key (e.g. PFMERGE dest). getKeysUsingKeySpecs treats that as invalid_spec, which discards all previously found keys and returns an error. Add pfmergeGetKeys as a getkeys callback so the command correctly falls back to it when key specs fail on the edge case.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches Redis key-extraction metadata for `PFMERGE`, which can affect tooling/cluster routing that relies on accurate key discovery, though the change is small and covered by a unit test.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes `COMMAND GETKEYS` for `PFMERGE` when called with only a destination key (no source keys), avoiding an "Invalid arguments" error.
> 
> This adds a dedicated `pfmergeGetKeys` callback, wires it into `PFMERGE` command metadata (both `commands.def` and `commands/pfmerge.json`), and adds a regression test covering both `PFMERGE dest src...` and `PFMERGE dest`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 1d27c533ff49e71af83ecad07eb7e9d8219b10e1. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Fix memory leak in lpBatchInsert()
body:
### Problem

In `lpBatchInsert()` (`src/listpack.c`), when `len > 3`, the function heap-allocates a temporary buffer of encoded entries:

```c
struct listpackInsertEntry tmp[3];   /* Encoded entries */
struct listpackInsertEntry *enc = tmp;

if (len > sizeof(tmp) / sizeof(struct listpackInsertEntry)) {
        /* If 'len' is larger than local buffer size, allocate on heap. */
        enc = zmalloc(len * sizeof(struct listpackInsertEntry));
}
```

The normal exit path at the bottom of the function correctly frees this buffer:

```c
if (enc != tmp) lp_free(enc);
return lp;
```

However, two early `return NULL` paths bypass this cleanup entirely:

**Leak path 1** — listpack size exceeds the 4 GB limit:
```c
if (new_listpack_bytes > UINT32_MAX) return NULL;  /* enc leaked */
```

**Leak path 2** — `lp_realloc()` fails (OOM):
```c
if ((lp = lp_realloc(lp, new_listpack_bytes)) == NULL) return NULL;  /* enc leaked */
```

In both cases, if `enc` was heap-allocated (`enc != tmp`), the memory is permanently leaked.

### Fix

Add the missing `if (enc != tmp) lp_free(enc)` cleanup before each early `return NULL`.
The `enc != tmp` guard ensures we only free heap allocations — when `len <= 3`, `enc` points to the stack buffer `tmp` and must not be freed.

### Testing & Verification

The fix is verified by code inspection: both `return NULL` statements skip the existing cleanup line. The `lp_free(enc)` mechanism already existed — it was simply unreachable on these paths.

**leak paths cannot be triggered easily:**:
will need A single listpack exceeding 4 GB of data, not practically reachable or Simulated OOM conditions.


Fixes #14864

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: small change limited to error/early-return paths in `lpBatchInsert`, only affecting cleanup on size overflow or `lp_realloc` failure.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes a memory leak in `lpBatchInsert()` by freeing the heap-allocated temporary `enc` buffer on early `return NULL` paths (listpack size would exceed `UINT32_MAX` or `lp_realloc()` fails). No functional changes to the success path; it only adds missing cleanup for these failure cases.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit fcfcbbe8f4f072f0d4da10dae1d30d7eb12f6464. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

i think they're wrong;
1. DEBUG POPULATE and a few others, have the value at hand, so they can pass it to
keyModified. It's harmless sine the key was just created and LRM is set, but its better for consistency to pass it anyway.

to avoid incrementing stats, key miss notification, and others.

> Touches core keyspace mutation paths (`RENAME`, `MOVE`, `COPY`, `DEBUG POPULATE`) and module APIs, which can affect eviction/LRM timestamps, client tracking invalidation, and keyspace stats if incorrect.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes several call sites to pass the actual value object into `keyModified()` after key creation/move/rename/copy, ensuring the LRM timestamp is updated for the destination key (instead of skipping LRM updates with `NULL`).
> 
> Updates `RM_SignalModifiedKey()` to use `lookupKeyReadWithFlags(..., LOOKUP_NOEFFECTS)` so signaling a modification doesn’t trigger read side effects like stats updates or key-miss notifications.

title: Fix memory leak in ZDIFF algorithm 2 on early exit
body:
## Problem

`zdiffAlgorithm2()` can break out early once the destination cardinality reaches zero. In that path, a temporary SDS created by `zuiSdsFromValue()` is left dirty and never released, because the cleanup normally happens on the next `zuiNext()` call which is skipped due to the early `break`.

`zuiClearIterator()` called after the loop does **not** clean up dirty values — only `zuiNext()` or explicit `zuiDiscardDirtyValue()` does.

## Root Cause

In `zdiffAlgorithm2()` (src/t_zset.c), the flow is:

1. `zuiSdsFromValue(&zval)` allocates an SDS string and sets `OPVAL_DIRTY_SDS` flag
2. If `cardinality == 0`, the code breaks out of the while loop
3. `zuiClearIterator()` is called but does not free the dirty SDS value
4. The allocated SDS is leaked

## Fix

Add `zuiDiscardDirtyValue(&zval)` before the early `break` to ensure the temporary SDS is freed on all exit paths.

## Tests Added

- **ZDIFF algorithm 2 empty result early exit**: Forces algorithm 2 selection by inflating `setnum` with non-existing keys, creates two identical sorted sets, runs `ZDIFFSTORE` which triggers algorithm 2's early exit when cardinality reaches 0, and verifies the result is empty. This test would expose the leak under Valgrind.

## Impact

Minimal scope change — one conditional block expanded. No behavioral change; only prevents a memory leak on a specific code path. The leak is triggered when ZDIFF(STORE) uses algorithm 2 and all elements of the first set are found in subsequent sets.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: small, localized memory-management fix in ZDIFF algorithm 2 plus a targeted regression test; no intended behavior change beyond preventing an SDS leak.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents a memory leak in `zdiffAlgorithm2()` by explicitly calling `zuiDiscardDirtyValue(&zval)` before breaking out of the removal loop when the destination cardinality hits zero.
> 
> Adds a unit test (`ZDIFF algorithm 2 empty result early exit`) that forces algorithm 2 selection with extra non-existent keys and verifies `ZDIFFSTORE` returns an empty result in the early-exit scenario.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ceec2065529bfa4da6a2d470f3f51db62cced788. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commits.message:
fix: memory leak in ZDIFF algorithm 2 on early exit

When zdiffAlgorithm2() breaks early because cardinality reaches zero,
the temporary SDS string created by zuiSdsFromValue() is not freed.
The zuiClearIterator() called after the loop does not clean up dirty
values — only zuiNext() or explicit zuiDiscardDirtyValue() does.

Add zuiDiscardDirtyValue(&zval) before the early break to prevent
the leak.

title: Fix stream_idmp_keys missing from database lifecycle ops
body:
**Summary**

Ensures `db->stream_idmp_keys` is managed consistently with `keys`, `expires`, and `subexpires` across every database lifecycle operation — flush, swap, temp-DB init/discard, lazy-free, and cluster slot migration.

Without this fix, the dict was absent from these code paths, causing three classes of bugs: a SIGSEGV during diskless replication in `swapdb` mode (NULL pointer dereference in `initTempDb`), silently lost IDMP tracking after `SWAPDB` and diskless replication (pointers never swapped), and stale IDMP entries surviving `FLUSHDB` (dict never cleared).

**Changes**

- **`emptyDbStructure`** (`src/db.c`) — Clear `stream_idmp_keys` on flush so stale entries don't persist across `FLUSHDB`/`FLUSHALL`.
- **`initTempDb` / `discardTempDb`** (`src/db.c`) — Create and release `stream_idmp_keys` for temp databases used during diskless replication RDB load. Fixes the SIGSEGV when `rdbLoadRioWithLoadingCtx` calls `dictAddRaw` on a NULL pointer.
- **`dbSwapDatabases`** (`src/db.c`) — Swap `stream_idmp_keys` alongside the other per-DB dicts so IDMP tracking follows the data during `SWAPDB`.
- **`swapMainDbWithTempDb`** (`src/db.c`) — Swap `stream_idmp_keys` when promoting a temp database after diskless replication, so the cron can discover and expire IDMP entries on replicas.
- **`streamMoveIdmpKeys`** (`src/db.c`) — New helper that migrates IDMP entries by slot from one dict to another, used during cluster slot migration.
- **`emptyDbAsync` / `emptyDbDataAsync`** (`src/lazyfree.c`) — Replace the dict with a fresh one and hand the old one to the background lazy-free job.
- **`lazyfreeFreeDatabase`** (`src/lazyfree.c`) — Free `stream_idmp_keys` in the background job (now takes 4 args instead of 3).
- **`asmTriggerBackgroundTrim`** (`src/cluster_asm.c`) — Move matching IDMP entries by slot into a temporary dict during background trim, then pass it to `emptyDbDataAsync` for async cleanup.
- **`server.h`** — Updated `emptyDbDataAsync` signature; added `streamMoveIdmpKeys` declaration.
- **Tests** (`tests/unit/type/stream.tcl`) — Four new integration tests covering IDMP expiry after `SAVE` + restart, tracking survival across `SWAPDB`, cleanup on `FLUSHDB`, and diskless replication in `swapdb` mode (both `rdbchannel=yes` and `rdbchannel=no`).

**What this fixes**

- **No crash on diskless replication** — `initTempDb` now initializes the dict, eliminating the NULL dereference during RDB load.
- **Tracking preserved across swaps** — Both `SWAPDB` and diskless replication correctly transfer the dict, so the cron keeps expiring entries in the right database.
- **Clean state after flush** — `FLUSHDB`/`FLUSHALL` clear the dict, preventing ghost entries from interfering with new streams.
- **Correct cluster migration cleanup** — IDMP entries for migrated slots are moved and freed alongside the key data.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core DB lifecycle paths (flush/swap/temp DB init/lazy-free/cluster slot trim) and stream IDMP tracking, which can impact replication/cluster correctness if mishandled, but changes are scoped to bookkeeping and include extensive tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes missing `stream_idmp_keys` handling so stream IDMP tracking is preserved/cleaned consistently across database lifecycle operations.
> 
> This wires `stream_idmp_keys` into `RESTORE`, overwrite/unlink/delete, `RENAME`/`MOVE`/`COPY`, `FLUSHDB`/`FLUSHALL`, `SWAPDB` and diskless replication temp-DB swap, plus async lazy-free cleanup and cluster background slot trimming (including a new `streamMoveIdmpKeys` helper).
> 
> Also updates stream duplication to copy IDMP producer state and adds broad integration tests covering expiry/cleanup and correctness across `SWAPDB`, flushes, rename/copy/move/restore, and diskless replication swapdb mode.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 78cfd58393be30b5f629606d56cbb5b2c4fdd457. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Fix heap-use-after-free in restoreCommand when module sets key metadata during notification
body:
### Problem

When a module's keyspace notification callback calls `RedisModule_SetKeyMeta` to attach metadata for the first time, `kvobjSet` reallocates the kvobj (to add a metadata slot) and frees the old one. The local `kv` pointer in `restoreCommand` becomes dangling, and the subsequent access to `kv->type` reads freed memory. 

Related PR: https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14918
Failed CI: https://github.com/redis/redis/actions/runs/23544446450/job/68540531298

### Changed

Save `kv->type` to a local variable before the notification calls.

CI result: https://github.com/vitahlin/redis/actions/runs/23575996441

**Note**: I’ve temporarily disabled `tests/unit/cluster/atomic-slot-migration.tcl` in the CI to avoid interference from #14901

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Small, targeted change to avoid dereferencing a potentially reallocated pointer; behavior is effectively unchanged aside from improved safety around module callbacks.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents a potential heap-use-after-free during `RESTORE` by caching `kv->type` into a local `kvtype` immediately after `dbAddInternal()`, and then using that cached value for subsequent type checks and `type_changed` notification comparisons.
> 
> Adds a clarifying note to `notifyKeyspaceEvent()` documenting that module notification callbacks can reallocate a key’s `kvobj`, making prior pointers unsafe to use after notifications.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 750b47c6fa19f7ddb97a29cf73ab65949ebc38a7. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: HSETNX notify after accessing KV
body:
On HSETNX command handling, the key space notification is sent before the complete usage of kvobject in stack unlike in the rest of the HSET* family of command handlers.

The problem is that when a Module writes potentially to Key Metadata, this KVObject may be reallocated and the usage of this after the notifciation becomes dangerous and can potentially lead to a crash.

This issue appeared because we started integration Key Metadata support on a module and observed that when handling HSETNX to update some metadata, we observed a crash.




<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches `HSETNX` mutation/notification ordering in core hash command handling; while behavior should be equivalent, it affects keyspace event timing and is exercised by modules, so regressions could impact integrations.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adjusts `hsetnxCommand` to emit the `"hset"` keyspace notification **after** all remaining uses of the `kvobj`, preventing stale-pointer crashes when notification callbacks (e.g., modules calling `RedisModule_SetKeyMeta`) trigger `kvobj` reallocation.
> 
> Adds a new test module (`keymeta_notify`) that mutates key metadata from keyspace notification callbacks and a new unit test (`ksn_notify_side_effect.tcl`) that stress-exercises hash, string, generic, expiry, and reload scenarios to ensure notifications with metadata side effects remain safe.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 7613c6020b1906ee8d5de299349ee0a720649838. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Fix missing ksn_notify_side_effect module test in runtest-moduleapi
body:
Fix missing `unit/moduleapi/ksn_notify_side_effect` in `runtest-moduleapi` test suite.

### Question: How about removing runtest-moduleapi entirely?

I noticed that `runtest-moduleapi` is rarely used and often gets out of sync because `runtest` and `make test` already cover all tests in `unit/moduleapi`.

In our CI environment, we always use the general test suite like this, we do not have a separate configuration for `runtest-moduleapi`:
https://github.com/redis/redis/blob/ef741a95a2338c95cc9a9fc193a5142d57c04fe2/.github/workflows/daily.yml#L58-L69





After a global search for moduleapi, I found that these are the only two places where `runtest-moduleapi` is still being used:
https://github.com/redis/redis/blob/ef741a95a2338c95cc9a9fc193a5142d57c04fe2/utils/releasetools/03_test_release.sh#L25-L28

https://github.com/redis/redis/blob/ef741a95a2338c95cc9a9fc193a5142d57c04fe2/src/Makefile#L509-L519

- The command in `03_test_release.sh` appear to be redundant
- If we need to keep this functionality, maybe we can just add an argument to make test for specific modules.

title: Fix VSIM FILTER memory leaks on duplicate option and error paths
body:
`VSIM_RedisCommand` in `vset.c` had two memory leak bugs related to the compiled FILTER expression (`exprstate` allocated by `exprCompile`):

1. Duplicate FILTER: When two FILTER options are provided in a single VSIM command, the second `exprCompile` overwrites `filter_expr` without freeing the first. Only the last one is freed in `VSIM_execute cleanup`. 
    Fix: call `exprFree` on the existing `filter_expr` before reassigning.

2. Error path leaks: When FILTER is parsed successfully but a later option fails validation (invalid COUNT/EF/EPSILON/FILTER-EF or unknown option), the error return frees `vec` but not `filter_expr`.
    Fix: add `exprFree(filter_expr)` to all five error return paths.

Added regression tests:
- tests/vsim_duplicate_filter.py
- tests/vsim_filter_error_leak.py


Without the fix, running the added tests with an ASan build will give lsan error for both tests:
```
==1416168==ERROR: LeakSanitizer: detected memory leaks

Direct leak of 16000 byte(s) in 200 object(s) allocated from:
    #0 0x79b64aab4887 in __interceptor_malloc ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_malloc_linux.cpp:145
    #1 0x5fb7f064008e in ztrymalloc_usable_internal .../redis/src/zmalloc.c:176
    #2 0x5fb7f064008e in zmalloc_usable .../redis/src/zmalloc.c:224
    #3 0x5fb7f064008e in RM_Alloc .../redis/src/module.c:530
    #4 0x5fb7f07d2972 in exprCompile ../modules/vector-sets/expr.c:595
    #5 0x5fb7f07e1126 in VSIM_RedisCommand ../modules/vector-sets/vset.c:1099
    #6 0x5fb7f0652c7f in RedisModuleCommandDispatcher .../redis/src/module.c:935
    #7 0x5fb7f030e922 in call .../redis/src/server.c:3926
    ...
```

Please let me know if I can provide any more information, thanks!

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches C-level command option parsing for `VSIM`; while behavior should be unchanged aside from memory cleanup, mistakes here could affect query handling or stability in the vector-sets module.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes memory leaks in `VSIM` when using the `FILTER` option by freeing any previously-compiled expression before compiling a new one, and by freeing the compiled expression on all option-parse/validation error returns (e.g., invalid `COUNT`/`EF`/`EPSILON`/`FILTER-EF` or unknown options).
> 
> Adds two regression tests that repeatedly issue `VSIM` with duplicate `FILTER` options and with a valid `FILTER` followed by invalid options, intended to catch these leaks under ASAN/valgrind.
>

commits.message:
Fix VSIM FILTER memory leaks on duplicate option and error paths

VSIM_RedisCommand in vset.c had two memory leak bugs related to the
compiled FILTER expression (exprstate allocated by exprCompile):

1. Duplicate FILTER: When two FILTER options are provided in a single
   VSIM command, the second exprCompile overwrites filter_expr without
   freeing the first. Only the last one is freed in VSIM_execute
   cleanup. Fix: call exprFree on the existing filter_expr before
   reassigning.

2. Error path leaks: When FILTER is parsed successfully but a later
   option fails validation (invalid COUNT/EF/EPSILON/FILTER-EF or
   unknown option), the error return frees vec but not filter_expr.
   Fix: add exprFree(filter_expr) to all five error return paths.

Added regression tests:
- tests/vsim_duplicate_filter.py
- tests/vsim_filter_error_leak.py

title: Test fix potential TCP deadlock in Active defrag IDMP streams test
body:
Failed daily CI: https://github.com/redis/redis/actions/runs/22980491707/job/66718992772


The IDMP streams defrag test sends all commands (100k) before reading any replies, which can cause TCP deadlock when buffers fill up.

Fix by batching writes and reads (1000 iterations per batch), consistent with the approach already used in the script defrag test above.



<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: changes are confined to a unit test and only adjust how replies are drained from a deferring client to avoid hangs in slow/TLS CI runs.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves the `Active defrag IDMP streams` test in `tests/unit/memefficiency.tcl` by **batching reads while enqueueing `XADD`/`SET` commands**, preventing TCP/congestion deadlocks when many deferred replies accumulate.
> 
> Replaces the single large reply-drain loop with periodic drains plus a final “remaining replies” drain, keeping test assertions and behavior the same while making CI runs less flaky.
>

title: Test fix ASM test failures under DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE builds
body:
Under `DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE`, every command triggers $O(n)$ full key scans, which causes ASM migrations to stall due to excessive per-command overhead on the destination node.This PR fixes the issue by skipping debug assertions during ASM import or background trimming


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: only changes when debug-only assertions run, gating them off during ASM import/trim; main runtime behavior is unaffected except for reduced assertion coverage during those operations.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents `dbgRunAssertions()` from running during ASM *import* in addition to existing background trim suppression, avoiding per-command O(n) key scans that can stall migrations under `DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE` builds and cause ASM test failures.
>

commits.message:
Add sleep to write load in ASM tests to allow replication lag convergence

Under DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE builds, every command triggers 3 full key
scans for assertions. Without sleep, the write load generates data at
a rate matching the destination's processing capacity, preventing
replication lag from converging below the handoff threshold. The
migration gets stuck forever and never reaches the handoff phase.

Add sleep=5ms to write loads in 'Migration will be successful after
fail points are cleared' and 'Source write pause timeout' tests.

Reduce populate_slot counts in ASM tests for DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE

Under DEBUG_ASSERT_KEYSPACE builds, every command triggers O(n) full
key scans. With 10000/5000 keys per slot, the per-command assertion
overhead (~12ms with 20000 total keys) exceeds the destination node's
processing capacity during migration, causing the migration to stall
permanently.

Reduce populate_slot from 10000 to 1000 and from 5000 to 500. These
counts still exercise the same code paths (RDB generation, streaming,
handoff, trim, digest comparison) as the tests verify logical
correctness, not scale.

Update comment src/db.c

Co-authored-by: Ozan Tezcan <ozantezcan@gmail.com>

- #14794 New `XIDMPRECORD` internal command and AOFRW emission to restore stream IDMP state
- #14816 setModuleEnumConfig() passing prefixed name to module callbacks

- #14855,#14831, #14817 Potential Memory leaks

> Bumps the release to **Redis 8.6.2** (version header + release notes), marking the release as *SECURITY* urgency and listing the included security/bug-fix PRs.

> Adds an integration test that reproduces the reported crash scenario by streaming diskless replication while continuously pipelining commands with `io-threads` enabled.

commits.message:
Skip memory prefetch during loading to avoid crash in dictEmpty callback (#14848)

Fixes #14838

## Summary

Fix a crash in `prefetchCommands()` that occurs during replica full sync
when the replica has existing data that needs to be emptied.

## Problem Description

During `emptyData()` → `kvstoreEmpty()` → `dictEmpty()` →
`_dictClear()`, the first hash table is cleared and `d->ht_table[0]` is
set to NULL via `_dictReset`. Then while clearing the second hash table,
every 65536 buckets it invokes `replicationEmptyDbCallback()` →
`processEventsWhileBlocked()` → `readQueryFromClient()` →
`prefetchCommands()`.
At this point, `dictSize() > 0` still holds (because the second hash
table isn't fully cleared yet), but `ht_table[0]` is already NULL. The
prefetch code assumed `ht_table[0]` is always valid when `dictSize() >
0`, leading to a crash.

## Solution
1. **Skip prefetch during loading**: Added a `server.loading` check at
the top of `prefetchCommands()` to return early. During RDB loading, the
main dictionary is being rebuilt, so prefetching keys from it is useless
anyway.
2. **Add defensive assertion**: Added
`serverAssert(batch->current_dicts[i]->ht_table[0])` in
`initBatchInfo()` to catch any future cases where `ht_table[0]` is NULL
while `dictSize() > 0` (which should only happen mid-`dictEmpty` via
`_dictReset`).

---------

Co-authored-by: kairosci <kairosci@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: debing.sun <debing.sun@redis.com>
Co-authored-by: Yuan Wang <yuan.wang@redis.com>

title: Skip memory prefetch during loading to avoid crash in dictEmpty callback
body:
Fixes #14838

## Summary

Fix a crash in `prefetchCommands()` that occurs during replica full sync when the replica has existing data that needs to be emptied.

## Problem Description

During `emptyData()` → `kvstoreEmpty()` → `dictEmpty()` → `_dictClear()`, the first hash table is cleared and `d->ht_table[0]` is set to NULL via `_dictReset`. Then while clearing the second hash table, every 65536 buckets it invokes `replicationEmptyDbCallback()` → `processEventsWhileBlocked()` → `readQueryFromClient()` → `prefetchCommands()`.
At this point, `dictSize() > 0` still holds (because the second hash table isn't fully cleared yet), but `ht_table[0]` is already NULL. The prefetch code assumed `ht_table[0]` is always valid when `dictSize() > 0`, leading to a crash.

## Solution
1. **Skip prefetch during loading**: Added a `server.loading` check at the top of `prefetchCommands()` to return early. During RDB loading, the main dictionary is being rebuilt, so prefetching keys from it is useless anyway.
2. **Add defensive assertion**: Added `serverAssert(batch->current_dicts[i]->ht_table[0])` in `initBatchInfo()` to catch any future cases where `ht_table[0]` is NULL while `dictSize() > 0` (which should only happen mid-`dictEmpty` via `_dictReset`).

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches the command prefetch path during replication/RDB loading; while the behavior change is small (early return), it affects a performance-critical hot path and adds a new invariant assertion that could trigger if other edge cases exist.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents a crash during replica full sync by **disabling `prefetchCommands()` while `server.loading` is true**, avoiding prefetching against dictionaries that may be mid-`dictEmpty`/reset.
> 
> Adds a defensive `serverAssert` in `initBatchInfo()` to ensure `ht_table[0]` is non-NULL when `dictSize() > 0`, and introduces an integration test that reproduces the replication + IO threads scenario with heavy pipelined traffic during a full resync.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 98914ba3a135750a88cdb66eb8c1628079edd352. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commits.message:
Fix: Add comprehensive NULL checks for prefetchCommands()

- Add NULL checks for individual argv elements before redis_prefetch_read()
- Add NULL checks before accessing ->encoding field
- Add bounds checking for key_pos in addCommandToBatch()
- Handle cases where argv is NULL or partially cleaned by IO threads
- Protect against accessing invalid pointers during replication

This prevents crashes when IO threads clean up partial argv arrays during RDB replication, particularly when modules send commands while the replica is loading the snapshot.

Fixes: #14838

Test: Enhance replication test for NULL argv handling

- Add comprehensive test for prefetchCommands() NULL argv handling
- Simulate IO thread cleanup scenarios during RDB replication
- Test with external module commands during snapshot loading
- Verify data consistency in NULL argv scenarios
- Test both normal and partial cleanup cases

This test exercises the real code path where the crash could occur:
prefetchIOThreadCommands() -> prefetchCommands() with IO threads active
during RDB streaming to the replica.

Related to: #14838

Fix TOCTOU races and missing NULL checks in prefetchCommands()

- Added local variable capture for pcmd->argv and pcmd->argv[j] to prevent TOCTOU crashes from concurrent I/O threads.
- Added !pcmd->argv check in addCommandToBatch().
- Improved integration test to correctly trigger a full sync and verify stability.

body:
3dfd99f85 2026-02-25 Add HOTKEYS HELP command coverage (#14814) (Mincho Paskalev)
ef69c2b9d 2026-03-18 Fix ACL OOB for wrong-arity KEYNUM commands (#14847) (Zijie Zhao)
625008c21 2026-03-13 Fix IDMP cron expiration not working after RDB load (#14869) (Sergei Georgiev)
5c14de015 2026-03-10 Fix X509 memory leak in connTLSGetPeerCert (#14855) (h.o.t. neglected)
455eb537f 2026-03-09 XADD, update keyModified and dirty counter when increasing iids_duplicates (#14858) (Oran Agra)
df55d1032 2026-03-06 Fix test assertion except from TSAN case (#14852) (Cong Chen)
faea84f43 2026-03-03 Fix setModuleEnumConfig() to pass unprefixed name to callbacks (#14816) (Moti Cohen)
1300c4459 2026-02-28 Don't use reply copy avoidance for module to prevent potential UAF (#14824) (debing.sun)
01774c763 2026-02-25 Fix implicit conversion warning in timeout.c for newer Clang versions (#14811) (Vitah Lin)
3a84fd1c0 2026-02-24 Add XIDMPRECORD command and AOFRW emission to restore stream IDMP state (#14794) (Sergei Georgiev)
f7cdc1607 2026-03-19 Fix FIELDS argument validation in HSETEX/HGETEX (#14883) (Cong Chen)
07e94036958af8f71c4ca5ad4a8464b99af86093 2026-03-19 Fix stream_idmp_keys missing from database lifecycle ops (#14897) (Sergei Georgiev)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches persistence/rewrite paths (AOF/RDB), stream internals, and DB lifecycle operations (swap/copy/move/flush/lazyfree), so bugs could impact data durability or background cleanup behavior; changes are targeted and covered by extensive new tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds internal stream command `XIDMPRECORD` (with command metadata/docs) to attach IDMP (producer/IID) metadata to an existing stream entry, and updates stream IDMP lookup to detect conflicts vs idempotent replays.
> 
> Updates AOF rewrite for streams to **preserve IDMP state** by emitting `XCFGSET` when per-stream IDMP config differs from defaults and emitting `XIDMPRECORD` entries (skipping references to deleted stream IDs). Fixes IDMP cron-expiration after RDB load and across DB lifecycle operations by tracking streams-with-IDMP in a new per-DB `stream_idmp_keys` dict that is maintained through load, delete/overwrite, `RENAME`, `MOVE`, `COPY`, `SWAPDB`, async flush/lazyfree, and cluster slot trim/migration.
> 
> Includes several smaller correctness/safety fixes: disable reply copy-avoidance for `RM_ReplyWithString` to prevent potential UAF during lazyfree, harden ACL key-spec/keynum handling and ensure arity is validated in module/Lua ACL checks, tighten hash-field-expire arg parsing (require single `FIELDS`), fix TLS peer cert leak, and adjust a few tests for stability/coverage (incl. `HOTKEYS HELP`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 07e94036958af8f71c4ca5ad4a8464b99af86093. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commits.message:
Add XIDMPRECORD command and AOFRW emission to restore stream IDMP state (#14794)

## Summary

- Introduce a new XIDMPRECORD <key> <pid> <iid> <streamID> command that
attaches IDMP (idempotency) metadata — a producer ID and an idempotency
ID — to an existing stream message. This is an internal command used to
replay IDMP state during AOF loading.
- Modify rewriteStreamObject() in AOF rewrite (AOFRW) to emit
XIDMPRECORD commands after the XADD and consumer-group commands,
ensuring IDMP deduplication state is fully preserved across AOF rewrites
and server restarts.
- Additionally emit XCFGSET during AOFRW when per-stream IDMP
configuration (IDMP-DURATION, IDMP-MAXSIZE) differs from server
defaults, so custom settings survive rewrites.

## New command: XIDMPRECORD
Syntax: `XIDMPRECORD <key> <pid> <iid> <streamID>`

- Validates that the key exists, is a stream, the stream ID refers to an
existing (non-deleted) entry, and both pid and iid are non-empty.
- If the (pid, iid) pair already maps to the same stream ID, the command
is idempotent and returns OK.
- If the (pid, iid) pair already maps to a different stream ID, an error
is returned.

## AOF rewrite changes (src/aof.c)

- New helper rioWriteStreamIdmpEntry() writes a single XIDMPRECORD bulk
command to the AOF rio.
- After consumer groups are emitted, iterates all producers in
s->idmp_producers and emits an XIDMPRECORD for every IDMP entry (in
linked-list insertion order).
- Emits XCFGSET for per-stream IDMP config when it diverges from server
defaults.

Fix implicit conversion warning in timeout.c for newer Clang versions (#14811)

## Problem

Build fails on macOS with Xcode 26.4 beta (Clang 21):

```
timeout.c:157:21: error: implicit conversion from 'long long' to 'long double' changes value from 9223372036854775807 to 9223372036854775808 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-const-int-float-conversion]
  157 |         if (ftval > LLONG_MAX) {
      |                   ~ ^~~~~~~~~
/Applications/Xcode_26.4_beta.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/clang/21/include/limits.h:109:20: note: expanded from macro 'LLONG_MAX'
  109 | #define LLONG_MAX  __LONG_LONG_MAX__
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
<built-in>:64:27: note: expanded from macro '__LONG_LONG_MAX__'
   64 | #define __LONG_LONG_MAX__ 9223372036854775807LL
      |                           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1 error generated.
make[1]: *** [timeout.o] Error 1
make: *** [all] Error 2
```
## Cause

Comparing `long double` with `LLONG_MAX` causes implicit conversion with
precision loss. Clang 21 has stricter warnings for this, and `-Werror`
treats it as an error.

## Fix

Add explicit cast to make the conversion intentional.

---------

Co-authored-by: debing.sun <debing.sun@redis.com>

Don't use reply copy avoidance for module to prevent potential UAF (#14824)

This PR disables reply copy avoidance for module strings.
The module string's lifecycle is not controlled by Redis, it may belong
to a datatype element that gets lazy-freed by BIO thread. If copy
avoidance holds a reference to it in the reply list, this causes UAF
when IO thread accesses the freed robj.

Fix setModuleEnumConfig() to pass unprefixed name to callbacks (#14816)

`setModuleEnumConfig()` was passing the prefixed config name to module
callbacks instead of the unprefixed name, inconsistent with other
config types. Fixed by using getRegisteredConfigName() like bool,
numeric, and string configs do.

Added assertions to all test module config callbacks to validate
correct unprefixed names are received.

Issue was introduced by #13656

Fix test assertion except from TSAN case (#14852)

Fix #14835

Executed `failover` with `force` argument in slow environments (TSAN, IO
threads, etc) can force a full resync instead of a partial one.
e3c38aab6 adds TSAN-only workaround in integration/failover test, test
failure occurs in `test‑ubuntu‑io‑threads` job, drop TSAN-only condition
and always check the sum of partial+full syncs will fix it.

Steps to reproduce:
My setup: Hardware: MacBook Pro (Apple chip)

1. Build docker image with Dockerfile below and run the container:
```
FROM ubuntu:22.04

# avoid interactive prompts
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    tcl8.6 \
    tclx \
    tcl-tls \
    pkg-config \
    ca-certificates \
    git \
    bash \
    vim \
    wget \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /opt/redis

# if you prefer to copy the source during build uncomment the next line
# COPY . /opt/redis

# configure a non-root user if desired; we stay root for simplicity

# default command keeps you in a shell
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

```

Execute the command:

```
cd /path/to/redis
docker build -f Dockerfile.io-threads -t redis-test-io .
docker run --rm -it --cpuset-cpus=0 --cpus=0.1 -v $(pwd):/opt/redis redis-test-io /bin/bash
```
2. Inside the container you can then run the same commands as the CI
job:
```
make REDIS_CFLAGS='-Werror'
./runtest --single integration/failover --config io-threads 4 --tags "failover" --verbose --accurate --loop --stop
```
If the container still seems too fast, lower `--cpus` further (e.g.
0.05), or run the whole driver under `cpulimit`:
```
apt-get update && apt-get install -y cpulimit
cpulimit -l 1 -- ./runtest --single integration/failover --config io-threads 4 --tags "failover" --verbose --accurate --loop --stop
```

XADD, update keyModified and dirty counter when increasing iids_duplicates (#14858)

we're saving iids_duplicates to the rdb file, so when it changes, we
need to increment the dirty counter, and also mark the key as modified
(being metadata, no signal is needed)

Fix X509 memory leak in connTLSGetPeerCert (#14855)

`connTLSGetPeerCert()` calls `SSL_get_peer_certificate()` which
increments the X509 reference count, but never calls `X509_free()` to
release it. This leaks an X509 object on each call to
`RM_GetClientCertificate()`. This PR adds `X509_free(cert)` before
returns.

The existing `test module getclientcert api` test in
`tests/unit/moduleapi/misc.tcl` exercises this code path. But the leak
was not caught because the ASAN CI job does not enable TLS.

Fix IDMP cron expiration not working after RDB load (#14869)

**Summary**

Registers streams with IDMP producers in `db->stream_idmp_keys` during
RDB load, so that the periodic cron job can expire stale IDMP entries
after a server restart. Without this fix, streams loaded from RDB were
never registered, causing IDMP entries to accumulate indefinitely and
never be cleaned up by the cron.

**Changes**

- **`rdbLoadRioWithLoadingCtx` (src/rdb.c):** After loading each
key-value pair, added a check for `OBJ_STREAM` type with a non-NULL
`idmp_producers` rax. When found, creates a string object for the key
and inserts it into `db->stream_idmp_keys` via `dictAddRaw`. If the key
already exists (duplicate insert), the reference is decremented to avoid
a leak.
- **Test `XADD IDMP cron expiration works after RDB load`
(tests/unit/type/stream.tcl):** Added an integration test that creates a
stream with IDMP producers, sets a short `IDMP-DURATION`, saves and
restarts the server, then verifies that the cron eventually expires the
entries (pids-tracked and iids-tracked drop to 0) and that
previously-expired IIDs can be re-added as new entries.

**Benefits**

- **Cron expiration works after restart:** Streams with IDMP producers
are now discoverable by the periodic cleanup cron after an RDB load,
ensuring expired entries are reaped as expected.
- **No memory leak on stale entries:** Without registration, IDMP
entries that outlived their duration would persist in memory forever
after a restart, growing unboundedly. This fix ensures they are cleaned
up.
- **Consistency between runtime and post-load behavior:** IDMP
expiration now behaves identically whether the stream was created at
runtime or restored from an RDB snapshot.

Fix ACL OOB for wrong-arity KEYNUM commands (#14847)

`luaRedisAclCheckCmdPermissionsCommand` and
`RM_ACLCheckCommandPermissions` now call `commandCheckArity()` to check
command arity before calling `ACLCheckAllUserCommandPerm`, matching the
behavior of `processCommand`, `scriptCall`, and `RM_Call`. Without this,
KEYNUM keyspec commands like EVAL with wrong arity cause out-of-bounds
argv access during key extraction.

Also fix KEYNUM index calculation (`first + keynumidx`) and add a bounds
check in genericGetKeys().

Add scripting and module ACL tests for wrong-arity `EVAL` to lock in the
non-crashing behavior.

Fixes #14843

Add HOTKEYS HELP command coverage (#14814)

Add coverage for HOTKEYS HELP command. Now reply schema test in Daily CI
does not fail.

Fix FIELDS argument validation in HSETEX/HGETEX (#14883)

Fixes #14879 

Improve validation of the FIELDS argument in HSETEX and HGETEX to ensure
exactly one field is provided, rejecting both missing and multiple
fields with consistent and accurate error messages.

Align behavior across both commands.

Fix stream_idmp_keys missing from database lifecycle ops (#14897)

**Summary**

Ensures `db->stream_idmp_keys` is managed consistently with `keys`,
`expires`, and `subexpires` across every database lifecycle operation —
flush, swap, temp-DB init/discard, lazy-free, and cluster slot
migration.

Without this fix, the dict was absent from these code paths, causing
three classes of bugs: a SIGSEGV during diskless replication in `swapdb`
mode (NULL pointer dereference in `initTempDb`), silently lost IDMP
tracking after `SWAPDB` and diskless replication (pointers never
swapped), and stale IDMP entries surviving `FLUSHDB` (dict never
cleared).

**Changes**

- **`emptyDbStructure`** (`src/db.c`) — Clear `stream_idmp_keys` on
flush so stale entries don't persist across `FLUSHDB`/`FLUSHALL`.
- **`initTempDb` / `discardTempDb`** (`src/db.c`) — Create and release
`stream_idmp_keys` for temp databases used during diskless replication
RDB load. Fixes the SIGSEGV when `rdbLoadRioWithLoadingCtx` calls
`dictAddRaw` on a NULL pointer.
- **`dbSwapDatabases`** (`src/db.c`) — Swap `stream_idmp_keys` alongside
the other per-DB dicts so IDMP tracking follows the data during
`SWAPDB`.
- **`swapMainDbWithTempDb`** (`src/db.c`) — Swap `stream_idmp_keys` when
promoting a temp database after diskless replication, so the cron can
discover and expire IDMP entries on replicas.
- **`streamMoveIdmpKeys`** (`src/db.c`) — New helper that migrates IDMP
entries by slot from one dict to another, used during cluster slot
migration.
- **`emptyDbAsync` / `emptyDbDataAsync`** (`src/lazyfree.c`) — Replace
the dict with a fresh one and hand the old one to the background
lazy-free job.
- **`lazyfreeFreeDatabase`** (`src/lazyfree.c`) — Free
`stream_idmp_keys` in the background job (now takes 4 args instead of
3).
- **`asmTriggerBackgroundTrim`** (`src/cluster_asm.c`) — Move matching
IDMP entries by slot into a temporary dict during background trim, then
pass it to `emptyDbDataAsync` for async cleanup.
- **`server.h`** — Updated `emptyDbDataAsync` signature; added
`streamMoveIdmpKeys` declaration.
- **Tests** (`tests/unit/type/stream.tcl`) — Four new integration tests
covering IDMP expiry after `SAVE` + restart, tracking survival across
`SWAPDB`, cleanup on `FLUSHDB`, and diskless replication in `swapdb`
mode (both `rdbchannel=yes` and `rdbchannel=no`).

**What this fixes**

- **No crash on diskless replication** — `initTempDb` now initializes
the dict, eliminating the NULL dereference during RDB load.
- **Tracking preserved across swaps** — Both `SWAPDB` and diskless
replication correctly transfer the dict, so the cron keeps expiring
entries in the right database.
- **Clean state after flush** — `FLUSHDB`/`FLUSHALL` clear the dict,
preventing ghost entries from interfering with new streams.
- **Correct cluster migration cleanup** — IDMP entries for migrated
slots are moved and freed alongside the key data.

title: Optimize rax insert and lookup for sequential key patterns
body:
**Summary**

Optimizes rax tree insert performance by reducing unnecessary reallocs and shortcutting sorted-array scans in `raxAddChild` and `raxLowWalk`, targeting the common case of sequential or append-heavy key insertion (e.g., stream IDs).

**Changes**

- **`raxAddChild` — skip realloc when allocation already fits**: Before reallocating the node, checks `rax_malloc_usable_size(n) < newlen`. Jemalloc size-class rounding often leaves enough usable bytes in the existing allocation to accommodate the extra child byte and pointer, making the realloc a no-op that can be avoided entirely.
- **`raxAddChild` — fast-path insertion position for append case**: When the new child character `c` is greater than the last existing child (`n->data[n->size - 1]`), sets `pos = n->size` directly instead of scanning the full sorted edge array. This is the hot path for sequential stream ID insertion where new keys are always lexicographically largest.
- **`raxLowWalk` — last-child-first lookup for non-compressed nodes**: Checks the last child in the sorted edge array before falling back to the linear scan. For sequential inserts the match is almost always at the tail. If the search byte is greater than the last child, breaks immediately (early miss), avoiding the full O(n) scan.

**Benefits**

- **Fewer reallocs**: Avoids `raxNodeRealloc` calls when the allocator already provided enough space, reducing allocator pressure and pointer-update overhead.
- **O(1) insert position for sequential keys**: The append fast-path in `raxAddChild` turns the child-position search from O(k) to O(1) for the dominant stream-ID pattern.
- **Faster tree walks**: The last-child-first check in `raxLowWalk` cuts the per-node scan cost for sequential workloads from O(k) to O(1), and adds at most one extra comparison for random workloads.
- **No memory overhead**: All optimizations operate on the existing node layout and sorted-children invariant. No additional fields, flags, or auxiliary data structures are introduced — memory usage is identical to before.
- **Zero behavioral change**: All three optimizations are purely performance shortcuts; no data structure or API semantics are altered.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches hot-path radix-tree insertion/lookup logic and relies on allocator usable-size semantics and ordering shortcuts; a bug could cause incorrect traversal or memory corruption, though changes are small and preserve existing invariants.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves rax performance for append-heavy/sequential key patterns by avoiding unnecessary work in `raxAddChild` and `raxLowWalk`.
> 
> `raxAddChild` now skips `raxNodeRealloc` when `rax_malloc_usable_size()` indicates the current allocation can already fit the grown node, and adds an O(1) fast-path to place new edges at the end when `c` is greater than the last child.
> 
> `raxLowWalk` accelerates non-compressed node traversal by checking the last child first and early-exiting when the search byte is greater than the last child, falling back to the existing linear scan otherwise.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit eafbabb89a59885f48e280b5dec344e2f805f51d. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: sggeorgiev

commits.message: added: rax insert optimizations - append fast-path and over-allocation in raxAddChild
added: rax fast-paths for sequential inserts in raxLowWalk
added: skip realloc in raxAddChild when usable block already fits

title: Modules KeyMeta (Keys Metadata)
body:
# TL;DR
Redis modules often need to associate additional metadata with keys in the keyspace. The objective is to create a unified and extensible interface, usable by modules, Redis core, and maybe later by the users, that facilitate the association and management of metadata with keys. While extending RedisModuleTypes might be an easier path, this proposal goes one step further: a general-purpose mechanism that lets attach metadata to any key, independent of underlying data type.

A major part of this feature involves defining how metadata is managed throughout a key’s lifecycle. Modules will be able to optionally register distinct metadata classes, each with its own lifecycle callbacks and capable of storing arbitrary 8-byte value per key. These metadata values will be embedded directly within Redis’s core key-value objects to ensure fast access and automatic callback execution as keys are created, updated, or deleted. Each 8 bytes of metadata can represent either a simple primitive value or a pointer/handle to more complex, externally managed data by the module and RDB serialized along with the key.

# Modules API
The core concept is to enable modules to "globally" register few key-metadata classes, and allowing to each class to attach metadata values to keys independently. We will limit the number of key-metadata classes registration to 8. The first one will be reserved for expiration. After registration it is possible for a module to set and get metadata values associated with specific keys using the new API.

Example:
```
/* Register key metadata Class */
RedisModuleKeyMetaClassConfig conf = {0};
conf.version = REDISMODULE_KEY_META_VERSION;
conf.flags = 1 << REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE;
conf.reset_value = (uint64_t)NULL;
conf.free = myKeyMetaFreeCallback;
conf.copy = myKeyMetaCopyCallback;
conf.rename = myKeyMetaRenameCallback;
conf.move = myKeyMetaMoveMoveCallback;
conf.unlink = myKeyMetaUnlinkCallback;
RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId kmcid = RedisModule_CreateKeyMetaClass(redisModuleCtx, "SRC1", MY_VERSION, &conf);

/* ... later on apply set & get on keys ... */
res = RedisModule_SetKeyMeta(kmcid, myRedisKey35, 0xFEDCBA9876543210 /*meta*/);
res = RedisModule_GetKeyMeta(kmcid, myRedisKey35, &meta); /*on return meta=0xFEDCBA9876543210*/
```
Initially, the concept was to support metadata embedding of arbitrary size. However, the increased processing overhead and API complexity might make this approach less practical. Instead, each metadata entry is now limited to a fixed size of 8 bytes per class, a compromise that balances efficiency and flexibility. These 8 bytes can also serve as a pointer or handle to externally allocated data when larger or more complex metadata is needed.

## RedisModule_CreateKeyMetaClass
Modules can define a new key-metadata class by invoking `RedisModule_CreateKeyMetaClass()`. Each metadata class is uniquely identified by a **4-character name** and a 5-bit encoding version, following the same naming and versioning conventions as module data types. However, key-metadata classes reside in their own dedicated namespace, ensuring there are no naming conflicts with existing module data types.

Redis allows up to eight key-metadata classes in total. One of these is reserved internally by Redis for key expiration, leaving up to seven available for use by modules. 
```
RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId RM_CreateKeyMetaClass(RedisModuleCtx *ctx,
                                                const char *metaname,
                                                int metaver,
                                                RedisModuleKeyMetaClassConfig *conf)
```

## RedisModule_ReleaseKeyMetaClass
Release a class by its ID. Note that its class-slot won't be freed since we can never know for sure (without scanning) whether there are keys with its metadata leftover. Nevertheless, this call can assist to identify such errors by exposing any remaining metadata references to the released class.

## RedisModule_SetModuleMetadata
Use this API to attach or update an 8-byte metadata value for a specific key under a specific metadata-key class (Add or update).
```
int RM_SetKeyMeta(RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId id, 
                  RedisModuleKey *key, 
                  uint64_t metadata)
```

# RedisModule_GetKeyMeta
```
int RM_GetKeyMeta(RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId id, 
                  RedisModuleKey *key, 
                  uint64_t *metadata)
```

## RedisModuleKeyMetaClassConfig
```
/* Key metadata class conf (Must be aligned with KeyMetaConfAllVersions) */
typedef struct RedisModuleKeyMetaClassConfig {
    /* Module need to set it REDISMODULE_KEY_META_VERSION. Bump when fields are
     * added; Redis keeps backward compatibility in RM_CreateKeyMetaClass(). */
    uint64_t version;

#define REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE 0 /* ignore meta on RDB load, if module not avail */
    uint64_t flags;

    /* Sentinel value meaning "no resource attached". It guarantees callbacks are 
     * ONLY invoked when meta != reset_value. This prevents double-free, avoids 
     * persisting sentinels to RDB/AOF, and simplifies module logic. */
    uint64_t reset_value;
    
    /* Copy callback (optional).
     * - Return 1 to attach `meta` to the new key, or 0 to skip attaching metadata.
     * - If NULL, metadata is ignored during copy.
     * - The `meta` value may be modified in-place to produce a different value
     *   for the new key. */
    int (*copy)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, uint64_t *meta);
    
    /* Rename callback (optional).
     * - If NULL, then metadata is kept during rename.
     * - The `meta` value may be modified in-place to produce a different value
     *   for the new key. */
    int (*rename)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, uint64_t *meta);
    
    /* Move callback (optional)
     *  - Return 1 to keep metadata, 0 to drop.
     *  - If NULL, then metadata is kept during move.
     *  - The `meta` value may be modified in-place to produce a different value
     *    for the new key. */
    int (*move)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, uint64_t *meta);

    /* Unlink callback (optional)
     * - If not provided, then metadata is ignored during unlink.
     * - Indication that key may soon be freed by bg thread.
     * - Pointer to meta is provided for modification. If the metadata holds a pointer
     *   or handle to resources and you free them here, you MUST set `*meta=reset_value`
     *   to prevent the free callback from attempting to free the same resource again. */
    void (*unlink)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, uint64_t *meta);
    
    /* Free callback (optional).
     * Invoked when a key with this metadata is deleted/overwritten/expired,
     * or when Redis needs to release per-key metadata during lifecycle ops.
     * (The module should free any external allocation referenced by `meta`
     * if it uses the 8 bytes as a handle/pointer). */
    void (*free)(const char *keyname, uint64_t meta);
    void (*aof_rewrite)(RedisModuleIO *aof, void *value, uint64_t meta);
    
    /* RDB load callback (optional).
     * - Called during RDB loading when metadata for this class is encountered.
     * - Behavior when NULL:
     *   > If rdb_load is NULL AND REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE flag is set,
     *     the metadata will be silently ignored during RDB load.
     *   > If rdb_load is NULL AND the flag is NOT set, RDB loading will fail
     *     if metadata for this class is encountered.
     * - Behavior when class is not registered:
     *   > If the class was saved with REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE flag but
     *     is not registered at load time, the metadata will be silently ignored.
     *   > Otherwise, RDB loading will fail.
     * - Callback responsibilities:
     *   > Read custom serialized data from `rdb` using RedisModule_Load*() APIs
     *   > Deserialize and reconstruct the 8-byte metadata value
     *   > Write the final 8-byte value into `*meta`
     *   > Return 1 to attach `meta` to the key, or 0 to skip attachment
     *   > database ID can be derived from `rdb` if needed. The associated key
     *     will be loaded immediately after this callback returns.
     * 
     * Parameters:
     * - rdb: RDB I/O context (use RedisModule_Load*() functions to read data)
     * - meta: Pointer to 8-byte metadata slot (write your deserialized value here)
     * - encver: Encoding version (the metadata class version at save time)
     * 
     * Return 1 to attach value `*meta` to the key. Or return 0 to ignore.
     */   
    int (*rdb_load)(RedisModuleIO *rdb, uint64_t *meta, int encver);

   /* RDB save callback (optional).
     * - If set to NULL, redis won't save metadata to RDB
     * - Callback should write RDB by functions: RedisModule_Save*()
     */
    void (*rdb_save)(RedisModuleIO *rdb, void *value, uint64_t *meta);
    
    /****************************** TBD: ******************************/    
    int (*defrag)(RedisModuleDefragCtx *ctx, RedisModuleString *keyname, uint64_t meta);
    size_t (*mem_usage)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, size_t sample_size, uint64_t meta);
    size_t (*free_effort)(RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, uint64_t meta);
} RedisModuleKeyMetaClassConfig;
```

# Flows
## Replica flow - Set metadata
Setting of metadata can be either initiated by Redis core Key Space Notification that will callback registered module or initiated by its own module command. In cas of KSN notification, the module will apply RM_SetKeyMeta() over a key and may optionally replicate the similar metadata operation to the replicas by function RM_Replicate(module-cmd-to-set-meta) such that it will be wrapped in atomic multi-exec together with the command that originally triggered the KSN, ensuring consistency across replicas. Otherwise, it can trivially call RM_Replicate(module-cmd-to-set-meta) to propagate the change.

# Redis internals 
## Extending kvobj
The kvobj structure (key-value object) is Redis's struct that holds both a key and its associated value. To support metadata, kvobj has been extended with optional, fixed-size metadata storage mechanism that maintains minimal memory overhead while providing fast access.

Metadata slots are allocated before the kvobj structure in memory, in reverse order of their class IDs. Each metadata class that is registered may attach to a given key 8 bytes of metadata value. The kvobj header itself contains a “metabits” field. An 8-bit bitmap where each bit indicates whether the corresponding metadata class (0-7) is active for this key.

```
Example with key "mykey" and expiration time (metabits=0b00000001):
    +--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------------+
    | Expiry Time  | serverObject | key-hdr-size | sdshdr5 "mykey" \0 |
    | 8 byte       | 16 bytes     | 1 byte       | 1      +   5   + 1 |
    +--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------------+
                   ^
                   +---- kvobjCreate() returns pointer here

Example with metadata classes 1 and 3 active (metabits=0b00001010):
+--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------------+
| meta (class3)| meta (class1)| serverObject | key-hdr-size | sdshdr5 "mykey" \0 |
| 8 byte       | 8 byte       | 16 bytes     | 1 byte       | 1      +   5   + 1 |
+--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------+--------------------+
                              ^
                              +---- kvobjCreate() returns pointer here   
```

## Keymeta Internal API
The keymeta subsystem provides a set of internal APIs used by Redis core and the module system to manage metadata classes and their lifecycle.

### keyMetaInit()
Initializes structure server.keyMeta.

### keyMetaClassCreate()
This function registers a new metadata class and returns a KeyMetaClassId. The registration process:
- Validates that the name is exactly 4 characters from charset A-Z a-z 0-9 _-
  - Stores short_name (4-char, e.g., "SRC1") for compact RDB serialization
  - Generates name (9-char, e.g., "META-SRC1") by adding "META-" prefix for runtime operations
  - Encodes the 9-char name and version into a 64-bit identifier using moduleTypeEncodeId() (54 bits for name + 10 bits for version)"
- Checks for name conflicts: Searches the server.keyMetaClass[] array for existing classes with the same name:
  - If a class with the same name is in CLASS_STATE_INUSE, registration fails (assertion in current implementation).
  - If a class with the same name is in CLASS_STATE_RELEASED.
Otherwise, find the first CLASS_STATE_FREE slot.
-  Stores the class configuration
- Marks the class as active: Sets the state to CLASS_STATE_INUSE.

### keyMetaSetMetadata()
This function optionally reallocates the kvobj to add the new slot. In case of reallocation, the function makes:
1. Preserves the key's position in the main dict (db->keys)
2. If needed, Preserves the expires dict entry, if the key has a TTL
3. If needed, Preserves Hash Field Expiration (HFE) state for hash objects
4. Calls function kvobjSet() with updated metabits to reallocate the object
5. Writes the new metadata value into the newly allocated slot
6. Updates main dict pointer to ref new allocation
7. If needed, restore expires dict pointer to ref new allocation
8. If needed, restore HFE state

### keyMetaGetMetadata()
Retrieves the 8-byte metadata value for a given key and class ID. 

### keyMetaClassRelease()
This step primarily serves to detect situations where a module is unloaded while keys still retain attached metadata. Such cases are undesirable, as Redis would lose access to the module’s callbacks that are essential for managing those metadata entries, potentially leading to undefined behavior.


## KeyMeta integration with core
The keymeta subsystem is integrated into core Redis operations:
- dbAddInternal(): Creates a new key with metadata specified by `keyMetaBits`
- kvobjCreate(): Allocates memory for metadata slots based on `keyMetaBits`
- kvobjSet(): Reallocates kvobj when `keyMetaBits` change
- RDB persistence: Metadata classes can provide rdb_save and rdb_load callbacks.
- AOF rewrite: Metadata classes can provide aof_rewrite callbacks.
- Defragmentation: Metadata classes can provide defrag callbacks (TBD).
- Memory usage: Metadata classes can provide mem_usage and free_effort callbacks (TBD).


## Serialization of Key Metadata
Serialization of key metadata in RDB files has been fully implemented and consists of two complementary components:
* Metadata class specification - Describing registered key-metadata classes, including: name, version, and flags
* Per-key metadata values - Storing the actual metadata values associated with each key

### Class Definition Serialization Strategy
Initially, we considered optimizing RDB size by serializing metadata class spec once (globally) and referencing them by index (1-7) in each key's metadata. However, this approach has a critical limitation: when RDB data is distributed to multiple targets, the global class registry might not be synchronized across targets. Therefore, we serialize the class spec along with each key's metadata, ensuring the metadata is self-contained and portable.

### Compact Encoding to Minimize RDB Size
While embedding class definitions with each key ensures correctness, it could significantly increase RDB file size for workloads with many keys using metadata. To mitigate this, we use a **compact 32-bit encoding** for the class specification (name + version + flags):
  - 6 bits × 4 chars = 24 bits for **short** name (charset: A-Z a-z 0-9 _-)
  - 5 bits for version (0-31)
  - 3 bits for flags:
      bit 0: REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE
      bits 1-2: Reserved for future use

This reduces the class specification in RDB from **8 bytes** (64-bit module-style 
encoding with 9-char name + 10-bit version) to just **4 bytes**, a **50% reduction**.

### RDB Format
**Current Key Serialization <TYPE, KEY, VALUE>**
```
1B: TYPE = RDB_TYPE_STRING      // Key type
?B: KEY = "myString"            // Key name (RDB string encoding)
?B: VALUE = "myValue"           // Key value (RDB string encoding)
```

**New Key Serialization <[META,] TYPE, KEY, VALUE>**
Metadata is serialized as an optional prefix before the key type, forming an integral unit that is parsed together by DUMP/RESTORE:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Optional Key Metadata Section ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1B: RDB_OPCODE_KEY_METADATA                      // New opcode
>   1B: NUM_CLASSES = 0x2                        // Number of metadata classes  
>   4B: CLASS_SPEC = {"FLX1" | v5 | flags=0x0}   // metaname + version + flags
>   ?B: VALUE                                    // Serialized by class's rdb_save() callback
>   1B: RDB_MODULE_OPCODE_EOF                    // End marker (verification)
>   4B: CLASS_SPEC = {"SRC3" | v13 | flags=0x0}  // metaname + version + flags
>   ?B: VALUE                                    // Serialized by class's rdb_save() callback
>   1B: RDB_MODULE_OPCODE_EOF                    // End marker (verification)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Standard Key Data ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1B: TYPE = RDB_TYPE_STRING                       // Key type
?B: KEY = "myString"                             // Key name (RDB string encoding)
?B: VALUE = "myValue"                            // Key value (RDB string encoding)
``` 

### Runtime Representation
To reuse existing module infrastructure (`moduleTypeEncodeId()`, entity lookup, etc.), each metadata class stores both representations in the `KeyMetaClass` structure:
  - **`short_name[5]`**: 4-character name for compact RDB serialization (e.g., `"FLX1"`)
  - **`name[10]`**: 9-character name with `"META-"` prefix for runtime operations (e.g., `"META-FLX1"`)

This dual-name approach:
- Maintains compact RDB representation (4 bytes per class specification)
- Reuses proven module type infrastructure (encoding, lookup, validation)
- Avoids namespace collision (metadata classes use separate namespace from module data types)"

### Note on Flags
The `REDISMODULE_META_ALLOW_IGNORE` flag is stored in the class's `KeyMetaClassConf.flags` at registration time among others. During RDB serialization, this flag is extracted and encoded into the 3-bit flags field of the CLASS_SPEC. During RDB deserialization, the flag determines whether to gracefully skip the metadata (if flag is set) or fail the RDB load when the metadata class is not registered.


# TODO
- Defragmentation: `defrag` callback support
- Memory usage: `mem_usage` and `free_effort` callback support

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Introduces a general-purpose per-key metadata framework usable by modules and core, with end-to-end persistence and lifecycle integration.
> 
> - Add `keymeta.c/.h` implementing metadata classes, `KeyMetaSpec`, lifecycle hooks (`copy/rename/move/unlink/free`), RDB (`rdbSaveKeyMetadata`/`rdbLoadKeyMetadata`) and AOF (`keyMetaOnAof`) handling
> - Extend `kvobj` to embed metadata (`metabits`) and accessors; adjust creation, set/expire, refcount free, and memory size (`object.c/h`)
> - Wire metadata through key ops: `dbAddInternal/dbAddRDBLoad`, `RENAME`, `MOVE`, `COPY`, and cluster `RESTORE` path; preserve HFE registrations where relevant
> - Persistence: new `RDB_OPCODE_KEY_META`, save/load in `rdb.c`, dump payload includes metadata in `cluster.c`, checker updated to skip metadata (`redis-check-rdb.c`)
> - Modules API: add `RM_CreateKeyMetaClass`, `RM_ReleaseKeyMetaClass`, `RM_SetKeyMeta`, `RM_GetKeyMeta`; refactor module I/O to `ModuleEntityId` and change `moduleInitIOContext` usage
> - Defrag: add `activeDefragKvobj` and integrate metadata-aware defragmentation paths (`defrag.c`)
> - Build/tests: include `keymeta.o` in server build and add `tests/modules/test_keymeta.so`; minor module type name/id field updates
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit f2cd817c225f56cb9d19f9fe7f42bb502b1dbe89. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commits.message: Adding AOF rewrite example and test
Use 4 chars instead of 9 for keymeta name

Implement reset_value checking for metadata callbacks

Redis now guarantees that metadata callbacks are only invoked when
meta != reset_value. This design provides several critical benefits:

1. PREVENTS DOUBLE-FREE: After RENAME/MOVE operations, source key's
   metadata is set to reset_value, preventing free callback from being
   invoked twice.

2. REDUCES UNNECESSARY CALLBACKS: Callbacks only invoked when there's
   actual metadata to process, avoiding wasted CPU cycles.

3. PREVENTS PERSISTING SENTINEL VALUES: rdb_save and aof_rewrite skip
   sentinel values, keeping RDB/AOF files clean.

4. SIMPLIFIES MODULE LOGIC: Module authors don't need to check for
   reset_value in callbacks - Redis guarantees valid metadata.

Changes:
- src/keymeta.c: Added design documentation and reset_value checks
  before all callback invocations (copy, rename, move, unlink, free,
  rdb_save, aof_rewrite)
- src/keymeta.h: Added concise comment explaining reset_value guarantee
- src/module.c: Updated API documentation for RM_CreateKeyMetaClass
  to explain the reset_value checking guarantee
- tests/modules/test_keymeta.c: Added comments clarifying that
  defensive checks are now redundant but demonstrate backward
  compatibility
FIX: Load with diff module registration preserves metadata correctly
Creating key metadata not during OnLoad should fail

title: Add global and per-command stats for slowlog metrics
body:
# What

Add global and per-command slowlog metrics.
`INFO STATS` now shows:
- slowlog_commands_count - total count of commands written to slowlog (including trimmed ones)
- slowlog_commands_time_ms_sum - sum of execution times of the commands from the slowlog.
- slowlog_commands_time_ms_max - maximum execution time of a command from the slowlog (useful for calculating averege values)

`INFO COMMANDSTATS` adds the equivalent 3 metrics, but per command. Only shown for a command if it was added at least once in the slowlog:
- slowlog_count - how many times the command was written in the slowlog
- slowlog_time_ms_sum - sum of execution time of the command (only from the slowlog)
- slowlog_time_ms_max - maximum execution time of the command (only from the slowlog)

# Why

More fine-grained slowlog metrics, easy of alert creation regarding slowlog.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core command execution/slowlog plumbing and extends `INFO` output; while behavior is additive, it changes an internal API (`slowlogPushEntryIfNeeded`) and adds new counters that must stay consistent under load.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds new **global** slowlog counters (count, sum, max duration) that are incremented when a command is actually written to the slowlog, reset by `CONFIG RESETSTAT`, and exposed in `INFO STATS` as `slowlog_commands_*`.
> 
> Adds **per-command** slowlog counters to `redisCommand` and conditionally appends `slowlog_*` fields to `INFO COMMANDSTATS` only for commands that have generated slowlog entries.
> 
> Updates `slowlogPushEntryIfNeeded` to return whether an entry was added, and expands unit tests to validate the new `INFO`/`COMMANDSTATS` metrics and adjust existing `cmdstat` assertions to tolerate extra fields.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 1cf302036b7d4caea8e077613df0fb2a843167c4. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
